steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between two independent clauses: one ends with a complete subject and verb, and so does the next. The question tests how to correctly separate two independent clauses.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "learning supervised": no punctuation between the two independent clauses — this is a run-on sentence. ✗
  • "learning, supervised": a comma alone between two independent clauses is a comma splice. ✗
  • "learning. Supervised": a period correctly ends the first independent clause and starts a new sentence. ✓
  • "learning, which supervised": turns the second clause into a relative clause fragment, distorting meaning and leaving no main verb. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
